How To Make DeFi Great Again | Adrian Cachinero Vasiljevic & Luca Prosperi
Quantum Could Break Bitcoin Sooner Than We Thought | Alex Pruden
How 3 CEOs Use AI to Run $10B in Companies | This Week in AI
podcasts.summary: Three CEOs managing a combined ~$10B in company value discuss how AI is reshaping enterprise infrastructure across tabular data (Fundamental's LTM/Nexus model for fraud detection, demand forecasting, etc.), AI video for business (Synthesia, 100M+ ARR, 90% of Fortune 100), and photonic computing (Light Matter, using light instead of copper to 3x AI training speeds). The broader theme is that AI is now automating cognition itself — not just physical labor — and most people are underestimating how fast the shift is happening.
keyTakeaways: LLMs haven't solved structured/tabular data — Fundamental is building a purpose-built Large Tabular Model (LTM) with different architecture than transformers, targeting enterprise use cases like fraud detection, demand forecasting, and ETA prediction that still run on pre-LLM ML algorithms.

OpenAI shutting down Sora signals a strategic retreat to focus: the lesson is that 'doing too many things at once' kills execution, and B2B copilot/coding is the near-term value driver — Claude Code is the talk of founder circles right now.

Photonic interconnects (fiber/light vs. copper) are the new Moore's Law bottleneck — Light Matter claims 3x faster AI training times, meaning early adopters at foundation model companies will compound their advantage dramatically.

The cognitive automation moment is underappreciated: 70% of workers think AI will hurt others' jobs but only 30% worry about their own — a dangerous blind spot as white-collar/knowledge work (including accounting) is now directly in scope.

AI video for business is a proven wedge: Synthesia's bet that enterprises want video-native communication (not docs/slides) has paid off — 100M ARR with 90% Fortune 100 penetration by focusing narrowly rather than chasing Hollywood-quality generation.
